request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We need to output a JSON with verdicts for each candidate. The instructions: "Accept an item only if it is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence." So we evaluate each candidate based on whether it is a valid, useful piece of operational knowledge from the guideline. The evidence quotes and source pages are provided. We need to check if the summary is accurate and the item is indeed operational knowledge (sequences, parameter specs, diagnostic rules, etc.) and not something like front matter or duplicate or unsupported.

We have 14 candidates. Let's go through each.

cand_dee654382aeb7562: Title: "Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode", section 5.13.4, knowledge_type: parameter_spec. Summary: The active setpoints vary based on Zone Group operating mode. Evidence: quote from guideline, includes table. Seems valid operational knowledge: it specifies how endpoints change with mode. Relevant. Accept.

cand_6b0905676f71b05b: Title: "Cooling Mode Cold Duct Airflow Reset", section 5.13.5.1.a. Summary: In cooling mode, cooling loop resets airflow setpoint from min to max, damper modulates. Evidence: direct quote. This is an operational sequence. Accept.

cand_7f59dd9a9a65cc25: Title: "Deadband Mode Cold and Hot Damper Control", section 5.13.5.1.b. Summary: In deadband, cold airflow setpoint is min endpoint, cold damper maintains it, hot damper closed. Evidence: quote from multiple pages. Operational sequence. Accept.

cand_52c48413354a8e0b: Title: "Heating Mode Hot Damper Close when Supply Air Colder than Room", section 5.13.5.1.c.1. Summary: If hot duct supply temp < room temp, close hot damper. Evidence: quote. Operational sequence. Accept.

cand_d964d0409a5dd352: Title: "Heating Mode Maximum Hot Duct Airflow Limiting Loop", section 5.13.5.1.c.2. Summary: Max hot duct airflow limited by reverse-acting P-only loop with heating max setpoint, output max damper position 0-100%. Evidence: quote. Operational sequence. Accept.

cand_b0b46c3609d42350: Title: "Override Close Heating Damper if Heating AHU Not Proven", section 5.13.5.2.a. Summary: If heating air handler not proven ON, close heating damper. Evidence: quote from multiple pages. Operational sequence. Accept.

cand_435fc1af8d48b38b: Title: "Override Close Cooling Damper if Cooling AHU Not Proven", section 5.13.5.2.b. Summary: If cooling air handler not proven ON, close cooling damper. Evidence: quote. Operational sequence. Accept.

cand_69fb799b173d6326: Title: "Low Airflow Level 4 Alarm", section 5.13.6.1.a. Summary: Generate Level 4 alarm if measured airflow <70% setpoint for 10 min while setpoint >0. Evidence: quote from many pages. This is a fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_dc7f13cb38b5f7ae: Title: "Low Airflow Level 3 Alarm", section 5.13.6.1.b. Summary: Generate Level 3 alarm if measured airflow <50% setpoint for 10 min while setpoint >0. Evidence: quote from pages. Fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_8c645d87a5aa30e6: Title: "Low Airflow Alarm Suppression for Non-Critical Zones", section 5.13.6.1.c. Summary: Alarms suppressed for zones with Importance-Multiplier 0 for static pressure reset. Evidence: quote. Fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_3a33d3ecd71f8522: Title: "Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm", section 5.13.6.2. Summary: If fan OFF and airflow reading >10% max or 50 cfm for 30 min, Level 3 alarm. Evidence: quote. Fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_23fd4288afdf358e: Title: "Leaking Damper Alarm", section 5.13.6.3. Summary: If damper at 0% and airflow >10% of cooling max or 50 cfm for 10 min while fan ON, Level 4 alarm. Evidence: quote. Fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_75ffabb44b4a8221: Title: "Airflow Alarm Threshold Determination", section 5.13.6.3. Knowledge_type: application_guidance. Summary: The constant thresholds for alarms should be set based on minimum accuracy of transducer and A/D converter. Evidence: quote from pages. This is guidance on configuring alarm thresholds. It is operational knowledge because it provides practical advice for implementation. Accept.

cand_8806ba8e5f4d1c63: Title: "Testing/Commissioning Overrides for Dual-Duct VAV Zone", section 5.13.7. Knowledge_type: commissioning_step. Summary: Provide software switches to force airflow setpoints, damper positions, reset accumulators. Overrides interlock at Zone Group level. Evidence: quote. This is a commissioning step, which is part of operational knowledge for testing. It seems valid. Accept.

All candidates appear to be valid, useful ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. No front matter, TOC, generic definitions, duplicates, or unsupported summaries. The evidence supports the summaries. So for each, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge should be true, and category_if_not null.

Thus, the output will have 14 verdicts, each with "is_valid_guideline36_knowledge": true, "reason": brief, "category_if_not": null.

I'll provide a concise reason for each, like "Valid parameter specification" or "Valid operational sequence", etc.

Now, format as JSON.
